positional_bias.note: max_share equals threshold (0.40), not strictly greater than; PASS. Sidecar distribution [1,3,0,2,1] confirmed against re-derived .gift parse — no desync.

length_bias.method: character length of answer text with #feedback suffix stripped

per_question.correct_text: A list where every vocabulary token gets a probability, and all probabilities sum to 1
A vocabulary of ~50,000–100,000 tokens covers all text by combining word parts well
Read all tokens in context, compute a fresh vocabulary distribution, then sample one token
The claim is wrong about the LLM — it samples from a distribution, so outputs can vary
Each run samples fresh from the distribution, so a different early token cascades forward

length_bias.note: Cycle-2 rebuild trimmed correct-answer text and balanced distractor lengths. All 5 MCQs pass. Rate 0.00 vs cycle-1 rate 1.00.

weakest_questions.question: q2
weakest_questions.distractor: Tokens are simpler for screens to display than full words or whole phrases are
weakest_questions.note: Unrelated to corpus rationale for tokenisation but not absurd; a beginner could imagine this as a reason.

lo_mapping.text: Explain in plain language what a probability distribution over a vocabulary is and why an LLM produces one at each step.
Describe what a token is and why LLMs work on tokens rather than whole words or whole sentences.
Trace the three-stage loop — take in context, compute a distribution, pick a token — and explain what happens at each stage.
Explain the difference between a deterministic system (always gives the same answer) and a stochastic system (can give different answers), and identify which category LLMs belong to.
Give one everyday analogy and one LLM-specific example that illustrate why the same prompt can produce different outputs on different runs.

beginner_fairness.notes: No temperature questions (deferred to 7.5 per corpus). No transformer internals. All terms (token, vocabulary, distribution, deterministic, stochastic) defined in corpus before use. All stems and distractors grounded in corpus sections 5.1–5.5.

verdict: Pass — all five checks clean. Positional bias at boundary (max_share=0.40, not >0.40; PASS). Length bias eliminated in cycle-2 rebuild (0/5 longest-correct, down from 5/5 in cycle 1). Zero absurd distractors (absurd_rate=0.00). Full 1:1 LO coverage, no orphans. No out-of-scope questions.
